ops.text:
LEAN-PREP lane RESULT (re-dispatch, GATE 2a + GATE 1 waiver) — CHECK-BACK. No arms run; TEST untouched. Two independent, each-sufficient blockers.

BLOCKER A — the lean invocation BREAKS the instrument (refutes the ruling's premise that scaffolding overhead "cancels in the paired comparison / irrelevant to the science"). Lean knob confirmed achievable: spawnllm ClaudeConfig(tools=(), system_prompt=JUDGE_SYSTEM) strips the built-in tool scaffolding (default plan already passes --system-prompt ""; the ~52k input was TOOL schemas + an agentic multi-turn loop, not a preamble), + env MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0 (thinking off, verified none in outputs). Had to shim a spawnllm 0.6.2 bug: build_command emits ["--tools", cfg.tools] without unpacking the tuple → empty tuple crashes subprocess (repo HEAD fixed). Re-baseline on the FIXED dev-50: lean split sonnet-v6-vs-auditor = 0.74, FAILS the amended harness-validity check (0.74 < stored-opus-v6-vs-auditor 0.82). Isolation run (Variant A = strip tools only, judge stays in user turn, identical rendering to scaffolded): 0.78 — still < 0.82. Decomposition: tool-strip costs 6 pts (scaffolded 0.84 → A 0.78, 18% verdict drift), the system/user split costs 4 more (→ 0.74, +8%). The scaffolded judge's built-in-tools agentic loop (scaffolded mean out=1220 / some 4869 multi-turn vs lean out=270 single-shot) was LOAD-BEARING for agreement. No config is both cheap and valid: keeping tools keeps 0.84 but pays scaffolded prices ($0.083/call, the 10-20x-over-cap the ruling already rejected).

BLOCKER B — cost still busts $18 even lean. Measured lean sonnet cost/call = $0.02927 (split, dev-50 mean; scaffolded $0.0826). Step-5 projection (dev 50×(1+3+8) sonnet + 50 opus verifier @ ~$0.15 + cheapest-arm SC-3 test 241×4 sonnet), incl. true spend-to-date $8.57: PROJECTED DESIGN ≈ $61.85 (3.4x over $18). Even DEV-ONLY lean ≈ $33.63 (1.9x over). Dominated by GenRM 8-sample fan-out + 50 opus verifiers; the per-call lean saving (2.8x) cannot close a 3.4x gap. NB the ledger.jsonl est_usd overcounts (charges cache-reads at full input rate: $8.68 for the scaffolded 50 vs true $4.13); budget tracked by true backend.accounting cost_usd, consistent with "~$4 PREP".

OPTIONS for orchestrator/user (driver has no discretion here — validity-floor relax + cap raise are both above the driver): (1) raise cap AND run at scaffolded prices to preserve the 0.84 valid instrument (accepts 10-20x; the registered "raised-cap follow-up is a user decision"); (2) shrink design to fit $18 at scaffolded (e.g. drop GenRM, SC-3-vs-baseline dev-only) — but even scaffolded SC-3 dev ≈ 200×$0.083 + $8.6 ≈ $25, still over, and erodes power; (3) relax the harness-validity floor to accept the lean 0.74/0.78 judge as a new weaker instrument (ruling-level decision, not the driver's); (4) close E34 dev-signal-only / abandon TTC-over-triage as untestable within $20 without either degrading the instrument below validity or busting the cap. Persisted: out/lean/baseline.jsonl (split, 0.74), out/lean/baselineA.jsonl (tools-only, 0.78); harness lean.py/lean_baseline.py/lean_variantA.py/analyze_lean.py.
